Conflict: The CND/CNG shall prevent the user from manually modifying the association between a public identity and a specific private identity corresponding to a single customer device not provided with a UICC.

Original_FR: The CNG and devices shall support mechanisms to identify and share information about the customer network devices (e.g. type of device and basic capabilities services supported etc.) within the customer environment. Original_FR: The CNG and devices shall support mechanisms to identify and share information about the customer network devices (e.g. type of device and basic capabilities services supported etc.) within the customer environment. Original_FR: The CNG and the Customer Network shall support the NGN services usage (e.g. voice communications video communications IPTV entertainment ?-) allowing the connection to the right service control platforms.

Original_FR: The CNG and the Customer Network shall support the NGN services usage (e.g. voice communications video communications IPTV entertainment ?-) allowing the connection to the right service control platforms.

Original_FR: The CNG may act as a proxy for handling messages (requests and responses) related to the remote access scenarios to and from the home.

Original_FR: The CNG may act as a proxy for handling messages (requests and responses) related to the remote access scenarios to and from the home.

Original_FR: The CNG may provide Home to Home (H2H) communication service connecting CNDs in different CPNs.

Conflict: When a parameter in the CNG Remote Management system is not part of the specific managed set, the system shall display such a parameter in red.

Original_FR: The CNG Remote Management requires to manage a specific set of parameters. At least the following list of parameters shall be available: Customer Identification Physical Line Identification list of subscribed services CNG IP address.

Original_FR: The CNG Remote Management requires to manage a specific set of parameters. At least the following list of parameters shall be available: Customer Identification Physical Line Identification list of subscribed services CNG IP address.

Original_FR: The CNG Remote Management requires to manage a specific set of parameters. At least the following list of parameters shall be available: Customer Identification Physical Line Identification list of subscribed services CNG IP address.
Conflict: If the CNG configuration profile is selected, the Remote Management system shall select all parameters from the master configuration database, sort the parameters by their internal identifier, and create the XML configuration file.

Original_FR: The CNG Remote Management requires to manage a specific set of parameters. At least the following list of parameters shall be available: Customer Identification Physical Line Identification list of subscribed services CNG IP address.

Original_FR: The CNG shall allow the customer remote access to a CND in the Customer Network for example a surveillance video camera.

Original_FR: The CNG shall allow the customer remote access to a CND in the Customer Network for example a surveillance video camera.

Conflict: The CNG shall restrict the assignment of IP address schemes and subnets such that each physical LAN port can only support a single, predefined scheme and subnet, irrespective of the operational mode, to enforce network segmentation.

Original_FR: The CNG shall support different IP address schemes and subnets on the same physical LAN port and on different LAN ports irrespective of routed or bridged mode of operation allowing the direct addressability of CNDs from the NGN side in relation to data plane control plane and management plane flows.
Conflict: If the NGN initiates a connection to a CND, the system shall use a single, pre-configured IP address and subnet mask for the LAN port, determined by the initial system configuration.

Original_FR: The CNG shall support different IP address schemes and subnets on the same physical LAN port and on different LAN ports irrespective of routed or bridged mode of operation allowing the direct addressability of CNDs from the NGN side in relation to data plane control plane and management plane flows.

Original_FR: The CNG shall support the connection to a STB (or media gateway) both in a bridged or in a routed mode of operation.

Original_FR: The CNG shall support the connection to a STB (or media gateway) both in a bridged or in a routed mode of operation.

Original_FR: The CNG shall support use of one user identity on several terminals as well as to share one terminal with several identities.

Original_FR: The CNG shall support use of one user identity on several terminals as well as to share one terminal with several identities.

Original_FR: The Disputes System shall provide update access capability for authorized users of the application.

Original_FR: The Disputes System shall provide view access capability for authorized users of the application.

Original_FR: The Disputes System shall provide view access capability for authorized users of the application.

Original_FR: The Disputes System shall record the name of the user and the date for any activity that creates or modifies the disputes case in the system. A detailed history of the actions taken on the case including the date and the user that performed the action must be maintained for auditing purposes.
Conflict: The Disputes System must prevent any modification to the case history log after an action has been initially recorded.

Original_FR: The Disputes System shall record the name of the user and the date for any activity that creates or modifies the disputes case in the system. Conflict: When the disputes case is actively being modified by a user, the Disputes System shall hide all user identification and timestamps from the user interface to prevent bias during the review process.

Original_FR: The Disputes System shall record the name of the user and the date for any activity that creates or modifies the disputes case in the system. A detailed history of the actions taken on the case including the date and the user that performed the action must be maintained for auditing purposes.

Original_FR: The Disputes System will facilitate direct data entry of a dispute case via a user interface that supports real time responses to the users.

Original_FR: The Disputes System will facilitate direct data entry of a dispute case via a user interface that supports real time responses to the users.
Conflict: "The Disputes System shall load all dispute case data from a pre-populated XML batch file, validate the data, and then process the cases into the system."

Original_FR: The Disputes System will facilitate direct data entry of a dispute case via a user interface that supports real time responses to the users.
Conflict: "The Disputes System shall import dispute cases via a pre-formatted batch file, which is processed nightly to update the case database."

Original_FR: The Disputes System will facilitate direct data entry of a dispute case via a user interface that supports real time responses to the users.

Original_FR: The Disputes System will provide the user the ability to create or initiate a ticket retrieval request. Original_FR: The Disputes System will provide the user the ability to create or initiate a ticket retrieval request. As part of ticket retrieval creation process the system must prompt the user to enter all the required information to create the ticket retrieval request. The ticket retrieval request is a document that is sent to merchant inquiring the validity of a transaction.
Conflict: When a user initiates a ticket retrieval request and begins entering the required information, but navigates away from the process before submitting it, the Disputes System shall automatically save and submit the partial request after a 30-second timeout.

Original_FR: The Disputes System will provide the user the ability to create or initiate a ticket retrieval request. As part of ticket retrieval creation process the system must prompt the user to enter all the required information to create the ticket retrieval request. The ticket retrieval request is a document that is sent to merchant inquiring the validity of a transaction.
Conflict: The system shall allow any user with a CSR or Operations role to initiate a ticket retrieval request without being prompted for all required information, enabling the submission of incomplete requests for later completion by the system.

Original_FR: The Disputes System will provide the user the ability to create or initiate a ticket retrieval request. As part of ticket retrieval creation process the system must prompt the user to enter all the required information to create the ticket retrieval request. The ticket retrieval request is a document that is sent to merchant inquiring the validity of a transaction.
Conflict: The Disputes System shall submit the partial request only once and ensure submission occurs within 35 seconds of the user leaving the process.

Original_FR: The Disputes System will provide the user the ability to create or initiate a ticket retrieval request. As part of ticket retrieval creation process the system must prompt the user to enter all the required information to create the ticket retrieval request. The ticket retrieval request is a document that is sent to merchant inquiring the validity of a transaction.
Conflict: The Disputes System must provide users with the ability to enter the required information to create a ticket retrieval request only once, after which the request becomes locked and uneditable.

Original_FR: The Disputes System will provide the user the ability to create or initiate a ticket retrieval request. As part of ticket retrieval creation process the system must prompt the user to enter all the required information to create the ticket retrieval request. The ticket retrieval request is a document that is sent to merchant inquiring the validity of a transaction.
Conflict: If the ticket retrieval request is selected, the Disputes System shall automatically generate the request document using transaction data from the payment database, populate all required fields, and send the inquiry to the merchant.

Original_FR: The Disputes System will provide the user the ability to create or initiate a ticket retrieval request. As part of ticket retrieval creation process the system must prompt the user to enter all the required information to create the ticket retrieval request. The ticket retrieval request is a document that is sent to merchant inquiring the validity of a transaction.

Original_FR: The document-handling system (TIU) shall have boilerplate and cut and paste capabilities.
Conflict: When the user initiates the document creation process, the system shall lock all boilerplate and cut and paste functions until a document type is selected.

Original_FR: The document-handling system (TIU) shall have boilerplate and cut and paste capabilities.
Conflict: The document-handling system (TIU) shall provide users with the option to disable boilerplate and cut and paste capabilities.

Original_FR: The document-handling system (TIU) shall include Consults/Request Tracking, Discharge Summary, and Progress Notes.
Conflict: The document-handling system (TIU) shall require all users to complete a new mandatory training module on system security before creating any new documents.

Original_FR: The DPU compression software shall produce compressed Data Packages from uncompressed Data Packages.
Conflict: The DPU compression software shall process only uncompressed image data files to produce compressed Data Packages.

Original_FR: The DPU compression software shall produce compressed Data Packages from uncompressed Data Packages.
Conflict: "The DPU compression software shall produce compressed Data Packages with a file size that is no smaller than 90% of the original uncompressed Data Package."

Original_FR: The DPU compression software shall report errors to the DPU-CCM for inclusion in the DPU Housekeeping information.
Conflict: "The system shall restrict error reporting to the DPU Fault Management Unit only, which is the sole authorized agent for populating DPU Housekeeping information."

Original_FR: The DPU-1553 CSC shall address hardware modules as defined in document 1400-01, Company X Specification for the Company X Communication/Memory Module.
Conflict: "The DPU-1553 CSC shall address hardware modules using the logical addressing scheme defined in document 1400-02, Company X Standard for Module Interface Addressing."

Original_FR: The DPU-ADC CSC shall address hardware modules as defined in document 1400, Company X Specification for the Company X Communication/Memory Module.
Conflict: The DPU-ADC CSC shall provide users with the option to bypass hardware module addressing as defined in document 1400.

Original_FR: The DPU-ADC shall create a fixed analog conversion scan list containing the A DPU analog values (active values plus one ground reference value).
Conflict: The DPU-ADC shall provide users with the option to modify the analog conversion scan list during system operation.

Original_FR: The DPU-ADC shall create a fixed analog conversion scan list containing the A DPU analog values (active values plus one ground reference value).
Conflict: The DPU-ADC shall create a dynamic analog conversion scan list based on the current operational mode, selecting only the required subset of A DPU analog values for conversion.

Original_FR: The DPU-ADC shall provide an application program with a function to perform an alternating read/setup-start sequence which will read the A/D result setup on the last call store the result into the scan list, and setup the conversion for the next item in the scan list; then on the next call start the conversion which was setup during the last call.
Conflict: The DPU-ADC shall provide an application program with a function to perform a direct read command which will initiate a new conversion, wait for completion, and return the A/D result without modifying the scan list or affecting any pending setup operations.

Original_FR: The DPU-ADC shall provide an application program with a function to perform an alternating read/setup-start sequence which will read the A/D result setup on the last call store the result into the scan list, and setup the conversion for the next item in the scan list; then on the next call start the conversion which was setup during the last call.
Conflict: The DPU-ADC shall prevent application programs from performing alternating read/setup-start sequences, requiring instead that each conversion setup and result read operation be executed as independent, discrete function calls.

Original_FR: The DPU-ADC shall read from the SCM registers in B-bit words only.
Conflict: If the operational history report is selected, the THEMAS system shall read from the SCM registers in variable-length data packets and create the ASCII report file.

Original_FR: The DPU-ADC shall write to the SCM registers in B-bit words only.
Conflict: "The DPU-ADC shall accept register write commands in both B-bit and 2B-bit word formats depending on the target register address."

Original_FR: The DPU-BIT shall complete the BIT within A seconds.
Conflict: When the DPU-BIT cannot complete the BIT within A seconds, the system shall display a "BIT In Progress" status message.

Original_FR: The DPU-BIT shall complete the BIT within A seconds.
Conflict: The DPU-BIT shall allow operators to interrupt the BIT procedure before completion.

Original_FR: The DPU-BIT shall perform MIL-STD-1553B internal BIT.
Conflict: The DPU-BIT shall perform MIL-STD-1553B internal BIT using only data from the last operational cycle stored in the flight data recorder.

Original_FR: The DPU-BIT shall perform two write/read/compare tests on the RAM for the DCI Data Buffers.The first test shall write the address of each location to that location.The second test shall write the ones complement of the values used in the first test.
Conflict: The DPU-BIT shall perform write/read/compare tests on the RAM for the DCI Data Buffers using only predetermined pseudorandom test patterns.

Original_FR: The DPU-BIT shall perform two write/read/compare tests on the RAM for the DCI Data Buffers.The first test shall write the address of each location to that location.The second test shall write the ones complement of the values used in the first test.
Conflict: The DPU-BIT shall perform a single write/read/compare test on the RAM for the DCI Data Buffers using a predefined pseudorandom pattern sequence.

Original_FR: The DPU-BIT shall perform two write/read/compare tests on the RAM for the MIL-STD-1553B.The first test shall construct and write half-word (16-bit) values obtained by adding the upper and lower half-words of each each memory address.The second test shall construct and write the ones complement of the half-word values used in the first test.
Conflict: To perform the RAM test, the DPU-BIT shall use pre-compiled, static test patterns stored in non-volatile memory for all write/read/compare operations on the MIL-STD-1553B RAM.

Original_FR: The DPU-BIT shall perform two write/read/compare tests on the RAM for the MIL-STD-1553B.The first test shall construct and write half-word (16-bit) values obtained by adding the upper and lower half-words of each each memory address.The second test shall construct and write the ones complement of the half-word values used in the first test.
Conflict: The DPU-BIT shall perform a single comprehensive write/read/compare test on the RAM for the MIL-STD-1553B using standardized test patterns.

Original_FR: The DPU-BIT shall test the Error Detection and Correction (EDAC) on the Company X Communication/Memory Module by reading preprogrammed error locations in PROM.
Conflict: If the EDAC diagnostic report is selected, the DPU-BIT shall read the current system state from the main memory controller and create a summary log file.

Original_FR: The DPU-BIT shall test the Programmable Read Only Memory (PROM) by computing a checksum and comparing it to a checksum stored in PROM.
Conflict: The DPU-BIT shall test the Programmable Read Only Memory (PROM) by performing a cyclic redundancy check (CRC) calculation and comparing it to a CRC value stored in the system's non-volatile configuration memory.

Original_FR: The DPU-BIT shall test the Programmable Read Only Memory (PROM) by computing a checksum and comparing it to a checksum stored in PROM.
Conflict: The DPU-BIT shall compute the PROM checksum without accessing the stored reference checksum for comparison.

Original_FR: The DPU-BIT shall use the 1553 Driver CSC to perform MIL-STD-1553B internal BIT.
Conflict: The DPU-BIT shall use the independent BIT Processor CSC to perform MIL-STD-1553B internal BIT without requiring the 1553 Driver.

Original_FR: The DPU-BIT shall use the 1553 Driver CSC to perform MIL-STD-1553B internal BIT.
Conflict: The 1553 Driver CSC shall prohibit the DPU-BIT from performing MIL-STD-1553B internal BIT operations.

Original_FR: The DPU-BIT shall use the EEPROM Driver CSC when reading from and writing to the EEPROM.
Conflict: The DPU-BIT shall use the Memory Management Unit CSC when reading from and writing to the EEPROM.

Original_FR: The DPU-BIT shall use the EEPROM Driver CSC when reading from and writing to the EEPROM.
Conflict: The DPU-BIT shall provide system administrators with the option to bypass the EEPROM Driver CSC for emergency read/write operations.

Original_FR: The DPU-BOOT CSC shall attempt to complete its normal execution sequence following detection of any failure.
Conflict: The DPU-BOOT CSC shall halt its execution sequence immediately upon detection of a critical failure.

Original_FR: The DPU-BOOT CSC shall disable write-protection of the EEPROM on the SCM.
Conflict: The DPU-BOOT CSC shall maintain write-protection of the EEPROM on the SCM at all times.

Original_FR: The DPU-BOOT CSC shall enable EDAC on the SCM.
Conflict: The DPU-BOOT CSC shall provide operators with the option to disable EDAC on the SCM.

Original_FR: The DPU-BOOT CSC shall include a CPU BIT consisting of a branch test, a fixed point test, a floating point test, an interrupt test, a timer test, and a register test.
Conflict: If the CPU BIT is initiated, the DPU-BOOT CSC shall execute only the branch test and the register test to minimize the boot sequence duration.

Original_FR: The DPU-BOOT CSC shall include a DRAM BIT consisting of two write/read/compare tests.The first test shall write the address of each memory location to that location.The second test shall write the complement of each memory location to that location.
Conflict: The DPU-BOOT CSC shall provide users with the choice to skip individual DRAM BIT write/read/compare tests during system initialization.

Original_FR: The DPU-BOOT CSC shall include a DRAM BIT consisting of two write/read/compare tests.The first test shall write the address of each memory location to that location.The second test shall write the complement of each memory location to that location.
Conflict: During the execution of the DRAM BIT, the DPU-BOOT CSC shall illuminate an amber LED to indicate that the test is in progress and no other system functions shall be initiated.

Original_FR: The DPU-BOOT CSC shall include a DRAM BIT consisting of two write/read/compare tests.The first test shall write the address of each memory location to that location.The second test shall write the complement of each memory location to that location.
Conflict: "The DRAM BIT shall accept a pre-defined test pattern file as its input and execute a single write/read/compare test using the specified data values."

Original_FR: The DPU-BOOT CSC shall increment the BOOT_CNT parameter in the EEPROM each time the DPU-RTOS is copied from EEPROM to DRAM and executed.
Conflict: The DPU-BOOT CSC shall prevent modification of the BOOT_CNT parameter in the EEPROM to maintain boot cycle integrity.

Original_FR: The DPU-BOOT CSC shall initialize Error Detection and Correction (EDAC) on the RSC by clearing DRAM.
Conflict: The RSC shall initialize its DRAM using pre-loaded operational data to ensure rapid transition to an operational state.

Original_FR: The DPU-BOOT CSC shall initialize Error Detection and Correction (EDAC) on the RSC by clearing DRAM.
Conflict: The DPU-BOOT CSC shall provide the option to skip EDAC initialization on the RSC to preserve DRAM contents.

Original_FR: The DPU-BOOT CSC shall initialize the BIT_DATA in the SYSTEM_BLOCK to the incomplete/failure state prior to executing Stage 1 BIT.

Original_FR: The DPU-BOOT CSC shall provide a monitor which accepts commands over the RS-232 interface.
Conflict: If the system boot diagnostic mode is initiated, the DPU-BOOT CSC shall accept commands exclusively via the Ethernet management port and disable the RS-232 command interface.

Original_FR: The DPU-BOOT CSC shall provide the capability to automatically load a configuration of DPU-RTOS from EEPROM to DRAM and start its execution.
Conflict: The DPU-BOOT CSC shall provide users with the option to bypass automatic loading of DPU-RTOS configuration from EEPROM and instead load from a network source.

Original_FR: The DPU-BOOT CSC shall provide the capability to automatically load a configuration of DPU-RTOS from EEPROM to DRAM and start its execution.
Conflict: If the manual boot override is selected, the DPU-BOOT CSC shall ignore the EEPROM configuration and load a default DPU-RTOS image from a specified network address to DRAM and start its execution.

Original_FR: The DPU-BOOT CSC shall put the processor into diagnostic mode.
Conflict: The DPU-BOOT CSC shall prevent the processor from entering diagnostic mode during normal operation.

Original_FR: The DPU-BOOT CSC shall read from EEPROM in 32-bit words only.
Conflict: The DPU-BOOT CSC shall read from EEPROM in 8-bit bytes only.

Original_FR: The DPU-BOOT CSC shall read from EEPROM in 32-bit words only.
Conflict: If the DPU-BOOT CSC is initiated in compatibility mode, it shall read the EEPROM data in 8-bit bytes to ensure correct operation with legacy hardware components.

Original_FR: The DPU-BOOT CSC shall set the processor speed  to MHz.
Conflict: When the system is operating in low-power mode, the DPU-BOOT CSC shall set the processor speed to 500 MHz.

Original_FR: The DPU-BOOT CSC shall set the processor speed  to MHz.
Conflict: If the system is in low-power standby mode, the DPU-BOOT CSC shall set the processor speed to 100 MHz.

Original_FR: The DPU-BOOT CSC shall test and clear DRAM on power-on using the COLD_MEM_SIZE obtained from the SYSTEM_BLOCK.
Conflict: The DPU-BOOT CSC shall skip DRAM testing and clearing on power-on to meet fast boot timing requirements.

Original_FR: The DPU-BOOT CSC shall test and clear DRAM on power-on using the COLD_MEM_SIZE obtained from the SYSTEM_BLOCK.
Conflict: "The DPU-BOOT CSC shall test and clear DRAM on power-on using the WARM_MEM_SIZE obtained from the RUNTIME_CONFIG block."

Original_FR: The DPU-BOOT CSC shall toggle the BC_INDEX (selecting the opposite configuration) after reading it during the boot process.
Conflict: The DPU-BOOT CSC shall preserve the BC_INDEX configuration (maintaining the current selection) after reading it during the boot process.

Original_FR: The DPU-BOOT CSC shall toggle the BC_INDEX (selecting the opposite configuration) after reading it during the boot process.
Conflict: The DPU-BOOT CSC shall maintain a fixed BC_INDEX configuration throughout the boot process without alteration.

Original_FR: The DPU-BOOT CSC shall write to EEPROM in 32-bit words only.
Conflict: The DPU-BOOT CSC shall write to EEPROM using 8-bit byte-addressable operations only.

Original_FR: The DPU-BOOT CSC shall write to EEPROM in 32-bit words only.
Conflict: The DPU-BOOT CSC shall accept configuration parameters as an 8-bit byte stream and write them directly to the EEPROM memory buffer.

Original_FR: The DPU-BOOT CSC X shall occupy no more than 32 KB of PROM.
Conflict: The DPU-BOOT CSC X shall write all boot sequence status messages to a dedicated 4 KB PROM log buffer.

Original_FR: The DPU-CCM shall be able to count a consecutively reported error.  When the the count for a particular error ID, exceeds 250 for a particular reporting period, the error code will be replaced with a error code sequence which shall include the original error code and the number of times the error was reported.
Conflict: The DPU-CCM shall provide operators with the option to disable the counting of consecutively reported errors for any selected error ID.

Original_FR: The DPU-CCM shall check the length of a received DPU_CMD against an expected value from a lookup table indexed by FUNCTION code.  If the received value does not match the expected, then the command will be discarded, and an error will be enqueued.
Conflict: "If the FUNCTION code corresponds to a dynamic configuration update, the DPU-CCM shall process the DPU_CMD regardless of its length, as the payload size is variable and defined within the command header."

Original_FR: The DPU-CCM shall collect a TASK_HBEAT from  DPU-SCUI, DPU-CCM, DPU-DCX, DPU-TMALI, and DPU-DPA .  Non-responsive tasks will be reported in DPU_HK.
Conflict: The DPU-CCM shall collect a TASK_HBEAT exclusively from DPU-SCUI and DPU-DCX. Heartbeats from other DPU components shall be ignored to reduce system overhead.

Original_FR: The DPU-CCM shall forward the DPU_HBEAT message to DPU-ICUI for transmission to the ICU at an interval of A seconds.
Conflict: "The DPU-CCM shall discard the DPU_HBEAT message when the system is in power-saving mode, regardless of the configured transmission interval."

Original_FR: The DPU-CCM shall implement a mechanism whereby large memory loads and dumps can be accomplished incrementally.
Conflict: The DPU-CCM shall require that all memory load and dump operations be executed as a single, atomic transaction.

Original_FR: The DPU-CCM shall implement a mechanism whereby large memory loads and dumps can be accomplished incrementally.
Conflict: The DPU-CCM shall process all memory load and dump operations as a single, atomic transaction that must complete in its entirety without interruption.

Original_FR: The DPU-CCM shall include the capability to read data from DRAM, the VME Bus, and the IOCC on ground command.
Conflict: The DPU-CCM shall read operational data exclusively from its internal non-volatile memory upon receiving any ground command.

Original_FR: The DPU-CCM shall interpret and execute/dispatch real-time DPU_CMDs.
Conflict: The DPU-CCM shall process DPU_CMDs in a non-real-time batch mode for system efficiency.

Original_FR: The DPU-CCM shall interpret and execute/dispatch real-time DPU_CMDs.
Conflict: "The DPU-CCM shall ignore incoming DPU_CMDs while performing internal memory diagnostics and cache validation routines."

Original_FR: The DPU-CCM shall monitor the status of DPU-SCUI, DPU-CCM, DPU-DCX, DPU-TMALI, and DPU-DPA by examining their TASK_INFO and report this information in DPU_HK.
Conflict: If the health status report is selected, the DPU-CCM shall select all the status data from the DPU-DCX log, sort the entries by component ID, and create the binary report file.

Original_FR: The DPU-CCM shall notify the ICU upon completion of boot and flight software initialization.
Conflict: The system shall allow any process with the ICU_VIEWER key to be recognized as the primary boot status notifier and have the authority to confirm flight software initialization.

Original_FR: The DPU-CCM shall produce DPU_HK at a commandable rate, with a default of 10 seconds.
Conflict: When the DPU-CCM is operating in power-saving mode, the platform shall suspend the production of DPU_HK.

Original_FR: The DPU-CCM shall provide a command to reboot the DPU.
Conflict: The DPU-CCM shall provide a command to reboot the DPU only when the system's operational status is "idle".

Original_FR: The DPU-CCM shall provide a command to resend DPU_HK_START.
Conflict: The DPU-CCM shall provide users with the choice to disable the DPU_HK_START resend command for an active session.

Original_FR: The DPU-CCM shall provide a command to resend DPU_HK_START.
Conflict: The DPU-CCM shall ignore any DPU_HK_START command while processing an active DPU_HK_STOP command.

Original_FR: The DPU-CCM shall provide the capability to echo to the spacecraft each received command from the spacecraft or the ICU.
Conflict: The DPU-CCM shall not echo commands received from the spacecraft or the ICU when operating in power-saving mode.

Original_FR: The DPU-CCM shall provide the capability to echo to the spacecraft each received command from the spacecraft or the ICU.
Conflict: If the command echo function is enabled, the DPU-CCM shall discard all incoming spacecraft commands without processing to prevent telemetry loopback.

Original_FR: The DPU-CCM shall record an error to the LAST_BOOT_IVEC location in EEPROM and discontinue strobing the Watchdog Timer should an unrecoverable software error occur.  An unrecoverable software error is defined as an error that causes a loss of commandability or ground communication.
Conflict: The DPU-CCM shall provide users with the choice to ignore unrecoverable software errors and continue strobing the Watchdog Timer to maintain system availability.

Original_FR: The DPU-CCM shall spawn tasks for DPU-CCM, DPU-DPA, DPU-DCX, DPU-SCUI, and DPU-TMALI at bootup.
Conflict: The DPU-CCM shall provide system administrators with the option to disable automatic spawning of DPU-DPA and DPU-DCX tasks during bootup.

Original_FR: The DPU-CCM shall use DPU-EEPRM to access the EEPROM.
Conflict: The DPU-CCM shall use the DPU-CCM internal memory buffer to access the EEPROM data.

Original_FR: The DPU-CCM shall use DPU-ICUI to communicate with the ICU.
Conflict: "The DPU-CCM shall use the RS-422 serial interface to communicate with the ICU."

Original_FR: The DPU-CCM shall utilize bootup default values stored in the SYSTEM_CONFIG_AREA in EEPROM when initializing the FSW
Conflict: If the DPU-CCM detects a valid configuration file on the startup SD card, it shall initialize the FSW using the parameters from this file and ignore the values in the EEPROM.

Original_FR: The DPU-DCI shall address hardware modules as defined in document 1400, Company X Specification for the Company X Communication/Memory Module.
Conflict: The DPU-DCI shall provide operators with the option to bypass hardware module addressing as defined in document 1400.

Original_FR: The DPU-DCI shall address hardware modules as defined in document 1400, Company X Specification for the Company X Communication/Memory Module.
Conflict: "The DPU-DCI shall address hardware modules as defined in the active configuration file, ignoring static definitions from document 1400."

Original_FR: The DPU-DCI shall provide access to the DCI status register so that an application program can determine error type.
Conflict: The DPU-DCI shall not provide access to the DCI status register so that error type information remains secure and inaccessible to application programs.

Original_FR: The DPU-DCI shall provide an application program with a function to read the contents of the ping/pong buffer.
Conflict: The DPU-DCI shall prevent an application program from directly reading the contents of the ping/pong buffer.

Original_FR: The DPU-DCI shall provide an application program with the ability to configure the SCM_DCI_WIN parameters.
Conflict: The DPU-DCI shall prevent an application program from modifying the SCM_DCI_WIN parameters after initial configuration.

Original_FR: The DPU-DCI shall provide an application program with the ability to force a ping-pong buffer swap when the interface is in Test Mode.
Conflict: The DPU-DCI shall prevent an application program from forcing a ping-pong buffer swap when the interface is in Test Mode.

Original_FR: The DPU-DCI shall provide an application program with the ability to read and write the hardware ping-pong buffers when in Test Mode.
Conflict: The DPU-DCI shall prevent an application program from writing to the hardware ping-pong buffers when in Test Mode.

Original_FR: The DPU-DCI shall provide an application program with the ability to read the current hardware status, including the Data Level Trigger, the Data Timeout Trigger, the DCI Event Mode, and the Normal/Test Mode.
Conflict: The DPU-DCI shall restrict an application program from reading the current hardware status, including the Data Level Trigger and the DCI Event Mode, to prevent unintended operational mode disclosure.

Original_FR: The DPU-DCI shall provide an application program with the ability to toggle the hardware interface between Normal mode and Test mode.
Conflict: The DPU-DCI shall prevent an application program from altering the hardware interface mode during runtime operation.

Original_FR: The DPU-DCI shall provide an application program with the capability to command the DCI hardware to reorder the bit sequence of each event's original Detector Electronics Output Format as it is received.
Conflict: The DPU-DCI shall prevent an application program from altering the bit sequence of any event's original Detector Electronics Output Format as it is received.

Original_FR: The DPU-DCI shall provide an application program with the capability to command the DCI hardware to reorder the bit sequence of each event's original Detector Electronics Output Format as it is received.
Conflict: "The DPU-DCI shall process each event's original Detector Electronics Output Format as it is received, using the hardware's native bit sequence without modification."

Original_FR: The DPU-DCI shall provide the capability for an application program to enable/disable the VME Slave Interrupt on the SCM for the DCI Interface.
Conflict: The DPU-DCI shall provide users with the choice to permanently lock the VME Slave Interrupt configuration for the DCI Interface after initial setup.

Original_FR: The DPU-DCI shall provide the capability for an application program to enable/disable the VME Slave Interrupt on the SCM for the DCI Interface.
Conflict: The DPU-DCI shall provide the capability for an application program to lock the VME Slave Interrupt configuration on the SCM for the DCI Interface.

Original_FR: The DPU-DCI shall provide the capability for an application program to install a callback routine to respond to the DCI Address Limit Interrupt Condition (INTADR_EN).
Conflict: The DPU-DCI shall prevent application programs from installing callback routines for the DCI Address Limit Interrupt Condition (INTADR_EN).

Original_FR: The DPU-DCI shall provide the capability for an application program to install a callback routine to respond to the DCI Error Interrupt (INTERR_EN).
Conflict: The DPU-DCI shall process the DCI Error Interrupt (INTERR_EN) exclusively through a fixed, statically-defined handler, prohibiting the installation of application-defined callback routines.

Original_FR: The DPU-DCI shall provide the capability for an application program to install a callback routine to respond to the Timeout Interrupt Condition (INTTO_EN).
Conflict: The DPU-DCI shall prevent application programs from installing callback routines for interrupt conditions to ensure deterministic system behavior.

Original_FR: The DPU-DCI shall provide the capability for an application program to mask/unmask each of the DCI Interrupt Conditions using the SCM_DCI_CSR.
Conflict: The DPU-DCI shall automatically mask all DCI Interrupt Conditions upon system initialization without application program intervention.

Original_FR: The DPU-DCI shall provide the capability for an application program to receive data at a rate of A bytes per second over the DCI interface.
Conflict: The DPU-DCI shall provide the capability for an application program to block incoming data at a rate of A bytes per second over the DCI interface.

Original_FR: The DPU-DCI shall provide the capability for an application program to set a timeout in milliseconds that specifies a period during which no new events have been received by the hardware, after which the hardware will swap ping-pong buffers and interrupt the software.
Conflict: The DPU-DCI shall prevent application programs from manually setting timeout periods, instead using a fixed, hardware-configured interval for buffer swapping and interrupt generation.

Original_FR: The DPU-DCI shall provide the capability for an application program to set the Address Limit at which the hardware will swap ping-pong buffers.
Conflict: The DPU-DCI shall prevent application programs from modifying the Address Limit at which the hardware will swap ping-pong buffers.

Original_FR: The DPU-DCI shall read from the DCI Memory in B-bit words only, using the load multiple/store multiple instructions.
Conflict: The DPU-DCI shall provide users with the option to read from the DCI Memory in variable bit-length words, using single load/store instructions.

Original_FR: The DPU-DCI shall read from the DCI Memory in B-bit words only, using the load multiple/store multiple instructions.
Conflict: "The DPU-DCI shall read from the DCI Memory in 32-bit words only, using the standard load word instruction."

Original_FR: The DPU-DCI shall read from the DCI Registers in C-bit words only
Conflict: The DPU-DCI shall provide the option to read from the DCI Registers in A-bit words only.

Original_FR: The DPU-DCI shall read from the DCI Registers in C-bit words only
Conflict: "The DPU-DCI shall read from the DCI Registers in 32-bit words only."

Original_FR: The DPU-DCI shall write to the DCI Memory in B-bit words only.
Conflict: The DPU-DCI shall read data from the DCI Memory in N-bit words, where N is a configurable parameter set by the host system.

Original_FR: The DPU-DCI shall write to the DCI Registers in C-bit words only
Conflict: If the DPU-DCI is in legacy mode, the DPU-DCI shall write to the DCI Registers in 16-bit words only.

Original_FR: The DPU-DPA shall be able to perform tracking based on data collected from the detector by calculating the spacecraft drift using successive tracking frames using the algorithm described in Appendix C.
Conflict: The DPU-DPA shall perform tracking by processing each frame independently without correlating data from successive frames.

Original_FR: The DPU-DPA shall be able to perform tracking based on data collected from the detector by calculating the spacecraft drift using successive tracking frames using the algorithm described in Appendix C.
Conflict: "The DPU-DPA shall be able to perform tracking using only the current tracking frame by calculating the spacecraft position relative to the detector's boresight, without using successive frames or historical data."

Original_FR: The DPU-DPA shall be capable of providing DPA_HK on request.
Conflict: The DPU-DPA shall provide users with the option to disable the DPA_HK provision feature for any request.

Original_FR: The DPU-DPA shall calculate drift with respect to the reference frame based on the established guide stars then perform a shift-and-add of the image mode data.
Conflict: The DPU-DPA shall perform a shift-and-add of the image mode data without calculating drift with respect to the reference frame.

Original_FR: The DPU-DPA shall calculate drift with respect to the reference frame based on the established guide stars then perform a shift-and-add of the image mode data.
Conflict: If the drift calculation is requested, the DPU-DPA shall use the latest inertial attitude estimate from the AOCS to perform a shift-and-add of the image mode data, bypassing the guide star matching process.

Original_FR: The DPU-DPA shall collect event mode data, preserving the time and spatial resolution in the specified event window.
Conflict: If the high-rate transient analysis is selected, the DPU-DPA shall collect a continuous data stream, averaging all event data over a one-second integration period.

Original_FR: The DPU-DPA shall command DPU-TMALI to reorder EVENT data bits as a function of the Detector Electronics Output Format (Science Mode 3, Full-Frame High Res), and the commanded DPU science or engineering mode.
Conflict: If the detector electronics output format is set to Science Mode 3, Full-Frame High Res, the DPU-DPA shall command DPU-TMALI to transmit the EVENT data bits in their raw, unmodified order to preserve the original signal fidelity.

Original_FR: The DPU-DPA shall perform bounds checking before accessing memory.
Conflict: The DPU-DPA shall allow memory access operations to proceed without bounds checking when operating in high-performance mode.

Original_FR: The DPU-DPA shall perform bounds checking before accessing memory.
Conflict: If the system is in a high-performance mode, the DPU-DPA shall access memory directly without performing bounds checks.

Original_FR: The DPU-DPA shall provide a task heartbeat to DPU-CCM.
Conflict: The DPU-DPA shall provide users with the option to disable the task heartbeat to DPU-CCM.

Original_FR: The DPU-DPA shall report errors to DPU-CCM.
Conflict: The DPU-CPM shall report errors to DPU-CCM.

Original_FR: The DPU-DPA shall transition MODE based on the protocol specified in document DPUICD-01, section 2.2
Conflict: The DPU-DPA shall allow the operator to manually override the automatic MODE transition specified in document DPUICD-01, section 2.2.

Original_FR: The DPU-DPA shall use the first tracking image accumulated after entering Image mode to locate guide stars using the algorithm described in Appendix C.
Conflict: The DPU-DPA shall provide operators with the option to bypass the first accumulated tracking image and manually select a subsequent image for guide star location.

Original_FR: The DPU-DPA shall use the first tracking image accumulated after entering Image mode to locate guide stars using the algorithm described in Appendix C.
Conflict: If the guide star catalog update is initiated, the DPU-DPA shall use the pre-loaded reference catalog instead of any newly accumulated tracking image to locate guide stars.

Original_FR: The DPU-DPA will complete the exposure in the time specified by the MODE command - even if it's required to truncate the final tracking frame.
Conflict: The DPU-DPA shall provide operators with the option to extend the exposure duration beyond the MODE command specification to ensure a complete final tracking frame.

Original_FR: The DPU-DPA will complete the exposure in the time specified by the MODE command - even if it's required to truncate the final tracking frame.
Conflict: "The DPU-DPA shall never truncate a tracking frame, even if this causes the exposure to exceed the time specified by the MODE command."

Original_FR: The DPU-DPA will obtain the initial tracking frame time from the ICU.
Conflict: If the tracking frame time initialization command is received, the DPU-DPA shall calculate the initial tracking frame time internally using its own system clock.

Original_FR: The DPU-EDAC shall address hardware modules as defined in document 1400, Company X Specification for the Company X Communication/Memory Module, and document IBM-FAULT-MGMT, RSC System: Fault Handling and Storage Management.
Conflict: The DPU-EDAC shall prevent direct addressing of hardware modules to maintain abstraction from the underlying hardware implementation.

Original_FR: The DPU-EDAC shall address hardware modules as defined in document 1400, Company X Specification for the Company X Communication/Memory Module, and document IBM-FAULT-MGMT, RSC System: Fault Handling and Storage Management.
Conflict: The DPU-EDAC shall address hardware modules as defined in document 1400, Company X Specification for the Company X Communication/Memory Module, and document IBM-FAULT-MGMT, RSC System: Fault Handling and Storage Management, and document ACME-HW-REV3, Third Party Hardware Interface Revision 3.

Original_FR: The DPU-EDAC shall contain a background task which walks DRAM to trip single-bit errors (SBEs).
Conflict: The DPU-EDAC shall provide system administrators with the option to disable the background DRAM walking task during high-priority operations.

Original_FR: The DPU-EDAC shall install an ISR to be invoked when an SBE occurs and scrub the location containing the SBE.
Conflict: The DPU-EDAC shall provide system administrators with the option to disable the automatic scrubbing of locations containing SBEs.

Original_FR: The DPU-EDAC shall record the current contents of EDAC_HK to EEPROM and force a DPU watchdog in the event an MBE in DRAM is detected.
Conflict: The DPU-EDAC shall continue normal operations without interruption in the event an MBE in DRAM is detected.

Original_FR: The DPU-EDAC shall record the number of MBEs that have been detected in DRAM since boot and the address of the last two MBEs.
Conflict: If the on-demand error report is generated, the DPU-EDAC shall clear the count of MBEs and the last two MBE addresses immediately after the report is created.

Original_FR: The DPU-EDAC shall record the number of MBEs that have been detected in PROM and EEPROM since boot and the address of the last two MBEs.
Conflict: The THEMAS system shall generate the operational history report using only the most recent 100 events from the event database to ensure a bounded report generation time.

Original_FR: The DPU-EDAC shall record the number of SBEs that have been detected in DRAM since boot and the address of the last two SBEs.
Conflict: If the DRAM error logging mode is enabled, the DPU-EDAC shall clear the SBE count and address log upon receiving the initialization command.

Original_FR: The DPU-EDAC shall record the number of SBEs that have been detected in PROM and EEPROM since boot and the address of the last two SBEs.
Conflict: The DPU-EDAC shall use a fixed-size circular buffer for SBE addresses, retaining only the most recent SBE address and discarding all previous addresses.

Original_FR: The DPU-EDAC shall report EDAC_HK on request.
Conflict: The DPU-EDAC shall never report EDAC_HK on request.

Original_FR: The DPU-ICUI shall check the length of a received DPU_CMD against the message length determined by the Inter-Block Gap.  If the received value does not match, then the command will be discarded, and an error will be enqueued to DPU-CCM, and a NAK message transmitted to the ICU within a second.
Conflict: The DPU-ICUI shall process all received DPU_CMD messages regardless of length discrepancies to ensure continuous data flow, and any validation against the Inter-Block Gap determined length shall be deferred to the application layer.

Original_FR: The DPU-ICUI shall compute a CHKS_16 for DPU_CMDs received from the ICU.  If the expected checksum is not computed, the command will be discarded, an error will be enqueued to DPU-CCM, and a NAK message transmitted to the ICU within a second.
Conflict: The DPU-ICUI shall process all DPU_CMDs from the ICU without checksum validation to maintain maximum throughput.

Original_FR: The DPU-ICUI shall format messages according to DPUICD-01, Section 3.1
Conflict: The DPU-ICUI shall allow users to disable message formatting according to DPUICD-01, Section 3.1.

Original_FR: The DPU-ICUI shall format messages according to DPUICD-01, Section 3.1
Conflict: "The DPU-ICUI shall format messages according to the real-time protocol specification in DPUICD-01, Appendix B."

Original_FR: The DPU-ICUI shall forward real-time commands to DPU-CCM within 500 milliseconds of receipt from the ICU.
Conflict: "The DPU-ICUI shall forward real-time commands to DPU-CCM within 500 milliseconds of receipt from the ICU, unless the system is operating in power-saving mode, in which case commands shall be queued and forwarded in batches every 5 seconds."

Original_FR: The DPU-ICUI shall install an ISR to be invoked on the SSI receiver interblock gap interrupt, for the receiving and processing of commands from the ICU.
Conflict: The DPU-ICUI shall provide system integrators with the option to disable the ISR for the SSI receiver interblock gap interrupt to optimize system performance.

Original_FR: The DPU-ICUI shall provide an API for other CSCs to transmit messages to the ICU.
Conflict: The DPU-ICUI shall provide an API that prevents other CSCs from transmitting messages to the ICU during system maintenance windows.

Original_FR: The DPU-ICUI shall provide ICUI_HK to the DPU-CCM on request.
Conflict: The DPU-ICUI shall not provide ICUI_HK to the DPU-CCM when the system is in power-saving mode.

Original_FR: The DPU-ICUI shall provide ICUI_HK to the DPU-CCM on request.
Conflict: The DPU-CCM shall operate autonomously without requesting ICUI_HK from the DPU-ICUI.

Original_FR: The DPU-ICUI shall provide ICUI_HK to the DPU-CCM on request.
Conflict: The DPU-CCM shall obtain ICUI_HK directly from the system database without requiring a request to the DPU-ICUI.

Original_FR: The DPU-ICUI shall use DPU-SSI to communicate with the SSI Interface.
Conflict: The DPU-ICUI shall use the Ethernet interface to communicate with the SSI Interface.

Original_FR: The DPU-ICUI shall use DPU-SSI to communicate with the SSI Interface.
Conflict: The DPU-ICUI shall provide operators with the option to disable DPU-SSI communication with the SSI Interface.

Original_FR: The DPU-RTOS shall be a real-time, multi-tasking operating system.
Conflict: The DPU-RTOS shall prevent users from modifying the real-time scheduling policy of any active task.

Original_FR: The DPU-RTOS shall exclude failed DRAM from the system memory pool based on the contents of the BIT_DRAM results in the SYSTEM_BLOCK.The system memory table does not include the Interrupt Vector Table (IVT), nor the text and data segment.
Conflict: "If the system memory initialization is performed, the DPU-RTOS shall include all available DRAM in the system memory pool based on the hardware-detected memory map, regardless of the BIT_DRAM results in the SYSTEM_BLOCK."

Original_FR: The DPU-RTOS shall include the capability to dynamically load object modules from the EEPROM.
Conflict: The DPU-RTOS shall load all required object modules from the EEPROM during system initialization and operate exclusively from RAM thereafter.

Original_FR: The DPU-RTOS shall provide a function to allow an application program to write to the Real-Time Clock registers on the RAD6000SC CPU Module.
Conflict: The DPU-RTOS shall prevent application programs from directly writing to the Real-Time Clock registers on the RAD6000SC CPU Module.

Original_FR: The DPU-RTOS shall provide the capability to invoke user-installed Interrupt Service Routines (ISRs) in response to internal and external (VME) interrupts.
Conflict: The DPU-RTOS shall provide users with the option to disable user-installed Interrupt Service Routines (ISRs) for specific interrupt sources.

Original_FR: The DPU-SCUI  shall be capable of packing created CCSDS Source Packets into STPDUs using segmented (grouped) packets for transfer to the SCU as defined by CCSDS-102.0-B-4 and in accordance with interface control document 1143-EI-S19121.
Conflict: The DPU-SCUI shall provide operators with the option to bypass STPDU packet segmentation and transfer CCSDS Source Packets as individual units to the SCU.

Original_FR: The DPU-SCUI  shall be capable of packing created CCSDS Source Packets into STPDUs using segmented (grouped) packets for transfer to the SCU as defined by CCSDS-102.0-B-4 and in accordance with interface control document 1143-EI-S19121.
Conflict: If the real-time telemetry stream is prioritized, the DPU-SCUI shall pack CCSDS Source Packets into individual, non-segmented STPDUs for immediate transfer to the SCU as defined by interface control document 1143-EI-S19122.

Original_FR: The DPU-SCUI  shall construct CCSDS Source Packets from Data Packages as defined by CCSDS-102.0-B-4, and in accordance with interface control document 1143-EI-S19121.
Conflict: The DPU-SCUI shall allow operators to bypass CCSDS Source Packet construction for Data Packages that fail validation checks.

Original_FR: The DPU-SCUI  shall construct CCSDS Source Packets from Data Packages as defined by CCSDS-102.0-B-4, and in accordance with interface control document 1143-EI-S19121.
Conflict: If the quick-look data summary is requested, the DPU-SCUI shall construct CCSDS Source Packets directly from raw instrument housekeeping telemetry, bypassing the standard Data Packages.

Original_FR: The DPU-SCUI  shall notify the SCU of new data by using the Transfer Request Counter in accordance with interface control document 1143-EI-S19121.
Conflict: If the operational history report is selected, the DPU-SCUI shall generate a notification to the SCU by immediately sending a dedicated service request message, bypassing the Transfer Request Counter mechanism.

Original_FR: The DPU-SCUI  shall provide SCUI_HK to the DPU-CCM on request.
Conflict: The DPU-SCUI shall not provide SCUI_HK to the DPU-CCM when the system is in power-saving mode.

Original_FR: The DPU-SCUI  shall provide SCUI_HK to the DPU-CCM on request.
Conflict: The DPU-CCM shall retrieve SCUI_HK directly from the system database without requiring a request to the DPU-SCUI.

Original_FR: The DPU-SCUI  shall provide the capability for data producing applications to specify whether or not queued data may be segmented.
Conflict: The system shall allow only users with the DATA_STEWARD role to have the authority to determine whether queued data may be segmented.

Original_FR: The DPU-SCUI  shall provide the capability for data producing applications to specify whether or not queued data may be segmented.
Conflict: The DPU-SCUI shall prevent data producing applications from specifying whether queued data may be segmented.

Original_FR: The DPU-SCUI  shall provide the capability to handle high priority data.  High priority data shall be processed before low priority data.  In the condition  that low priority data is being processed when new high priority data arrives, DPU-SCUI shall finish processing the low priority data and then  immediately shift all resources to processing the high priority data.
Conflict: The DPU-SCUI shall provide users with the option to immediately abort processing of low priority data when new high priority data arrives.

Original_FR: The DPU-SCUI shall be capable of receiving a telecommand from the SCU every N ms and forward it to DPU-CCM for processing.
Conflict: "The DPU-SCUI shall discard any telecommand received from the SCU within N ms of the previous one to prevent command queue overflow."

Original_FR: The DPU-SCUI shall buffer an amount of data specified by the SCU_BUFFER_SIZE parameter provided during initialization.The default buffer size will be set to X MB of data.
Conflict: "The DPU-SCUI shall process streaming data in real-time without buffering to ensure minimum latency for critical control signals."

Original_FR: The DPU-SCUI shall cancel the write to each subaddress if the SCU fails to read a pending STPDU after a timeout specified by a value calculated based on the SCU_POLL_RATE parameter provided during initialization, shall record new data to those subaddresses, and report an error to the DPU-CCM.The SCU_POLL RATE parameter shall have a default setting of J ms and shall be capable of being modified dynamically.
Conflict: The DPU-SCUI shall maintain all pending writes to subaddresses indefinitely until the SCU successfully reads the STPDU, regardless of timeout conditions, to ensure no data loss occurs during SCU communication interruptions.

Original_FR: The DPU-SCUI shall communicate with the SCU according to the protocol defined in document 1143-EI-S19121.
Conflict: The DPU-SCUI shall operate independently without requiring communication with the SCU.

Original_FR: The DPU-SCUI shall communicate with the SCU according to the protocol defined in document 1143-EI-S19121.
Conflict: "The DPU-SCUI shall communicate with the SCU using the protocol defined in the hardware configuration file loaded during system initialization."

Original_FR: The DPU-SCUI shall compute a CHKS16 for telecommands received from the SCU.  If the CHKS16 does not match the value included in the telecommand,  an error will be reported to DPU-CCM.  If the checksum computes correctly, the command will be forwarded to DPU-CCM for execution.
Conflict: The DPU-SCUI shall process telecommands from the SCU without performing a CHKS16 verification to minimize latency.

Original_FR: The DPU-SCUI shall detect the condition in which the SCU fails to read one or more MIL-STD-1553B subaddresses during the transmission of a STPDU, shall cancel the write on those subaddresses, record new data to those subaddresses, and report an error to the DPU-CCM.
Conflict: The DPU-SCUI shall ignore read failures on MIL-STD-1553B subaddresses during STPDU transmission and proceed with the write operation to maintain data flow continuity.

Original_FR: The DPU-SCUI shall rate-buffer the delivery of STPDUs to the SCU according to the SCU_BUFFER_RATE parameter provided during initialization.The default value shall provide data to the SCU at a rate of X Kbps and shall be capable of being modified dynamically.
Conflict: The DPU-SCUI shall deliver STPDUs to the SCU immediately upon reception, ignoring any rate-buffering configuration.

Original_FR: The DPU-SCUI shall rate-buffer the delivery of STPDUs to the SCU according to the SCU_BUFFER_RATE parameter provided during initialization.The default value shall provide data to the SCU at a rate of X Kbps and shall be capable of being modified dynamically.
Conflict: If the SCU is in power-saving mode, the DPU-SCUI shall deliver all buffered STPDUs in a single burst to minimize the SCU's active processing time.

Original_FR: The DPU-SCUI shall receive command messages in the form of Telecommand Packets from the SCU and make them available to the DPU-CCM.
Conflict: The DPU-SCUI shall receive command messages directly from the ground station interface and make them available to the DPU-CCM.

Original_FR: The DPU-SCUI shall report errors to the DPU-CCM for inclusion in the DPU Housekeeping information.
Conflict: The DPU-CCM shall independently detect and report all DPU errors for inclusion in the DPU Housekeeping information without relying on the DPU-SCUI.

Original_FR: The DPU-SCUI shall time stamp the secondary header of the Source Packets with the current SC_TIME retrieved from the DPU-TIS module.
Conflict: When the current SC_TIME retrieved from the DPU-TIS module is invalid or unavailable, the DPU-SCUI shall leave the timestamp field in the secondary header of the Source Packets blank.

Original_FR: The DPU-SCUI shall time stamp the secondary header of the Source Packets with the current SC_TIME retrieved from the DPU-TIS module.
Conflict: The DPU-SCUI shall use a fixed timestamp value in the secondary header of the Source Packets instead of the current SC_TIME from the DPU-TIS module.

Original_FR: The DPU-SCUI shall time stamp the secondary header of the Source Packets with the current SC_TIME retrieved from the DPU-TIS module.
Conflict: If the playback mode is initiated, the DPU-SCUI shall time stamp the secondary header of the Source Packets with the time value extracted from the recorded telemetry data.

Original_FR: The DPU-SCUI shall use DPU-1553 to communicate with the MIL-STD-1553B Interface.
Conflict: The DPU-SCUI shall use the Ethernet interface to communicate with the MIL-STD-1553B Interface.

Original_FR: The DPU-SCUI shall use DPU-1553 to communicate with the MIL-STD-1553B Interface.
Conflict: The DPU-SCUI shall provide users with the option to disable DPU-1553 communication with the MIL-STD-1553B Interface.

Original_FR: The DPU-SCUI, in the event of a buffer full condition, shall discard new data, return an error to the caller, and report an error condition to the DPU-CCM.
Conflict: "The DPU-SCUI, in the event of a buffer full condition, shall queue incoming data and retry the operation until successful, while continuing normal operations."

Original_FR: The DPU-SSI shall address hardware modules as defined in document 1400, Company X Specification for the Company X Communication/Memory Module.
Conflict: The THEMAS system shall address hardware modules as defined in the legacy interface specification, document 1200.

Original_FR: The DPU-SSI shall address hardware modules as defined in document 1400, Company X Specification for the Company X Communication/Memory Module.
Conflict: The DPU-SSI shall prevent direct hardware module addressing to enforce abstraction layers as defined in document 1500, Company X Specification for the Secure System Interface.

Original_FR: The DPU-SSI shall provide an application program with the capability to read the SSI status register.
Conflict: The DPU-SSI shall prevent an application program from reading the SSI status register.

Original_FR: The DPU-SSI shall provide an operational mode (Tx MESSAGE MODE) for transmitting blocks in which consecutive words in a block are transmitted,  and the transmitter is disabled until a transmit IBG interrupt is received.  At this point the transmitter is re-enabled, and the next message can be transmitted.
Conflict: "The DPU-SSI shall provide an operational mode (Tx CONTINUOUS MODE) for transmitting blocks where the transmitter remains permanently enabled and automatically processes consecutive messages without requiring interrupt-based re-enabling between transmissions."

Original_FR: The DPU-SSI shall provide the capability for an application program to enable/disable the VME Slave Interrupt on the SCM for the SSI Interface.
Conflict: The DPU-SSI shall provide the capability for an application program to permanently lock the VME Slave Interrupt configuration on the SCM for the SSI Interface.

Original_FR: The DPU-SSI shall provide the capability for an application program to install a callback routine to respond to the receive IBG Interrupt.
Conflict: The DPU-SSI shall provide users with the option to disable callback routine installation for the receive IBG Interrupt.

Original_FR: The DPU-SSI shall provide the capability for an application program to install a callback routine to respond to the SSI Error Interrupt.
Conflict: The DPU-SSI shall provide no mechanism for an application program to override the default SSI Error Interrupt handler.

Original_FR: The DPU-SSI shall provide the capability for an application program to install a callback routine to respond to the transmit IBG Interrupt.
Conflict: The DPU-SSI shall prevent application programs from installing callback routines for the transmit IBG Interrupt.

Original_FR: The DPU-SSI shall provide the capability for an application program to set the Inter-Block Gap (IBG) Timeout for transmitted and received messages.
Conflict: The DPU-SSI shall provide users with the choice to disable the Inter-Block Gap (IBG) Timeout configuration for transmitted messages.

Original_FR: The DPU-SSI shall provide the capability for an application to determine the number of bytes in a message received from the ICU.
Conflict: The DPU-SSI shall provide the capability for an application to determine the number of bytes in a message received from the ICU only if the message integrity check has passed.

Original_FR: The DPU-SSI shall read from the SSI registers in 16-bit words only.
Conflict: The DPU-SSI shall read from the SSI registers in 32-bit words only.

Original_FR: The DPU-SSI shall read from the SSI registers in 16-bit words only.
Conflict: "The DPU-SSI shall read from the SSI registers in 32-bit words only."

Original_FR: The DPU-SSI shall write to the SSI registers in 16-bit words only.
Conflict: "If the operational history report is selected, the THEMAS system shall select all the events from the event database, sort the events by date and time, and create the binary report file."

Original_FR: The DPU-TIS  shall provide TIS_HK to the DPU-CCM on request.
Conflict: The DPU-TIS shall not provide TIS_HK to the DPU-CCM when the system is in power-saving mode.

Original_FR: The DPU-TIS shall provide the capability for an application program to jam the value of the SC_TIME into the hardware, if automatic time synchronization is disabled.
Conflict: The DPU-TIS shall prevent an application program from modifying the SC_TIME value in the hardware under all circumstances.

Original_FR: The DPU-TIS shall provide the capability for an application program to select the active 1PPS signal.
Conflict: The DPU-TIS shall allow any user with a valid system login to be viewed as a timing administrator and have full authority to select the active 1PPS signal.

Original_FR: The DPU-TIS shall provide the capability for an application program to set the value of the SC_TIME register(s) on the SCM and enable the 1PPS clock jam register, if automatic time synchronization is enabled.
Conflict: The DPU-TIS shall prevent an application program from modifying the SC_TIME register(s) on the SCM or the 1PPS clock jam register while automatic time synchronization is enabled.

Original_FR: The DPU-TIS shall provide the capability for application programs to access the current UT_DELTA.
Conflict: The DPU-TIS shall provide users with the option to disable access to the current UT_DELTA for application programs.

Original_FR: The DPU-TIS shall provide the capability for application programs to access the current UT_DELTA.
Conflict: The DPU-TIS shall provide the capability for application programs to access the current UT_DELTA only when the system is in a non-operational state.

Original_FR: The DPU-TIS shall provide the capability for application programs to access the current UT_DELTA.
Conflict: The DPU-TIS shall provide application programs with the choice to ignore the current UT_DELTA value.

Original_FR: The DPU-TIS shall provide the capability for application programs to access the SC_TIME from the SCM.
Conflict: The DPU-TIS shall provide application programs with the option to disable access to the SC_TIME from the SCM.

Original_FR: The DPU-TIS shall read from the SCM registers in B-bit words only.
Conflict: The DPU-TIS shall read from the SCM registers using a 32-bit word size for status registers and a 16-bit word size for configuration registers.

Original_FR: The DPU-TIS shall read from the SCM registers in B-bit words only.
Conflict: The DPU-TIS shall read from the SCM registers in variable bit-length words.

Original_FR: The DPU-TMALI  shall provide TMALI_HK to the DPU-CCM on request.
Conflict: The DPU-CCM shall provide TMALI_HK to the DPU-TMALI on request.

Original_FR: The DPU-TMALI shall allocate a ring buffer of size specified by TMALI_BUFFER_SIZE provided during initialization for storing incoming frames of data.The default buffer size shall be 15 MB.
Conflict: The DPU-TMALI shall display a warning message indicating "Insufficient buffer space for operation" when the ring buffer utilization exceeds 90% of the TMALI_BUFFER_SIZE.
